content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Donald Donaldson 
460 Hazelwood Avenue 
San Francisco, CA 
94127 
Dear Mr. Donaldson: 
December 23, 1987 
RE: 
Your Request for Information 
Our File No. 1-87-302 
You have requested information regarding the campaign 
provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974.1I 
All your 
questions pertain to the use of campaign funds by Mayor Dianne 
Feinstein. 
The Political Reform Act of 1974, administered and 
interpreted by the Fair Political Practices Commission, 
requires that all contributions received and expenditures made 
by candidates, officeholders, and campaign committees be 
disclosed. 
The Act does not place any restrictions on the use 
or expenditure of such funds. 
However, the California 
Elections Code, section 12400, et seq. does address the use of 
campaign funds. 
The Elections Code is interpreted by the State Attorney 
General's office. 
You may obtain information by writing to the 
Department of Justice, 1515 K Street, suite 511, Sacramento, 
CA 
95814. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Grif 'ths 
Gen 
al 
/ ~~ 
~~_ 
By: 
aaten-Moen 
Reform Consultant 
lIGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California 
Administrative Code section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California 
Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660 




California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
December 4, 1987 
Donald Donaldson 
400 Hazelwood Avenue 
San Francisco, CA 
94127 
Re: 
87-302 
Dear Mr. Donaldson: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on November 30, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.
